returns.tvl: TVL (total value locked) calculated from the prices and other pool data

computeTVLGivenPrices(int256[]).details: Prices are defined the same way as in the oracle feed, and scaled to 18-decimal FP values. Since it accepts arbitrary prices, this version isn't for use in production, but is a useful utility function for testing, integration, and simulation.

params.prices: An array of prices for the tokens in the pool, sorted in token registration order

returns.tvl: TVL (total value locked) calculated from the prices and other pool data

getFeedData().details: The feeds might have different update frequencies; e.g., one updates hourly, and another daily. Some use cases may require sophisticated analysis, so `updatedAt` returns all values.

returns.prices: An array of latest prices from the feeds
returns.updatedAt: An array of timestamps corresponding to the last update of each feed

returns.feedScalingFactors: An array of scaling factors corresponding to each feed

returns.feeds: An array of AggregatorV3Interface instances representing the feeds

returns.tokens: An array of token addresses, sorted in token registration order

getShouldUseBlockTimeForOldestFeedUpdate.details: If set, `latestRoundData` returns the current time for `updatedAt`, instead of calculating the minimum update time over all the feeds (i.e., using the update time of the "oldest" / most stale feed).

computeTVL().notice: Calculates the TVL based on the current prices.

computeTVLGivenPrices(int256[]).notice: Calculates the TVL based on the given prices.

getFeedTokenDecimalScalingFactors().notice: Gets the decimal scaling factors for each feed token.

getFeeds().notice: Gets the list of feeds used by the oracle.

getPoolTokens().notice: Gets the tokens in the pool.
